raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement focus mainly on prose and structure, or should it also include substantive changes to the paper's claims and argumentation?","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft may require reframing the contribution claim and clarifying what PhysiCollab adds beyond standard PINN loss weighting.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the stated architecture, agent framing, and claimed avoidance of gradient pathologies form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The draft makes empirical claims that should align with the reported Burgers and Allen-Cahn results if the text is improved substantively.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request can centrally include improving section-level narrative arc and ordering of claims.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for better prose, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection should preserve uncertainty about whether the user wants surface editing or substantive research revision.","RX.2":"The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported architecture and experiments support.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than executing edits to the draft.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific presentation or unsupported performance claims.","RX.5":"The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus mainly on prose and structure, or should it also include substantive changes to the paper's claims and argumentation?

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft may require reframing the contribution claim and clarifying what PhysiCollab adds beyond standard PINN loss weighting.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the stated architecture, agent framing, and claimed avoidance of gradient pathologies form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes empirical claims that should align with the reported Burgers and Allen-Cahn results if the text is improved substantively.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request can centrally include improving section-level narrative arc and ordering of claims.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for better prose, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty about whether the user wants surface editing or substantive research revision.
rationale.RX.2: The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported architecture and experiments support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than executing edits to the draft.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific presentation or unsupported performance claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
